title: Software Engineer
description:
**Overview**
 We are looking for a
 **Software Engineer** 
 to build and evolve a high‑quality consumer‑facing Windows application used by millions of users. You will work across the full product lifecycle 9design, development, experimentation, and production) partnering closely with Product Managers, Designers, and Data Science teams to deliver intuitive, performant, and reliable Windows experiences.
   

  

 This role emphasizes solid engineering fundamentals, customer empathy, and ownership of end‑to‑end feature delivery in a fast‑moving consumer environment.
   

  

 Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.
   

  

 Starting January 26, 2026, Microsoft AI (MAI) employees who live within a 50\\-mile commute of a designated Microsoft office in the U.S. or 25\\-mile commute of a non\\-U.S., country\\-specific location are expected to work from the office at least four days per week. This expectation is subject to local law and may vary by jurisdiction.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Design, build, and ship features for a consumer Windows application with a solid focus on usability, performance, reliability, and accessibility.
* Develop high‑quality, maintainable, and well‑tested code using modern Windows frameworks and tooling.
* Collaborate with Product, Design, and Data partners to translate customer needs into technical solutions.
* Participate in architecture and design reviews, contributing to decisions around scalability, responsiveness, and long‑term maintainability.
* Instrument features with telemetry, analyze usage and reliability signals, and iterate based on data and customer feedback.
* Debug and resolve issues across the stack, including live‑site and customer‑reported problems.
* Contribute to engineering excellence through code reviews, test coverage, CI/CD practices, and operational readiness.


**Qualifications**
**Required Qualifications:**
* Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, or related technical discipline with proven experience coding in languages including, but not limited to, C, C\\+\\+, C\\#, Java, JavaScript, or Python.
+ OR equivalent experience.

* Solid software engineering fundamentals in C\\+\\+, C\\#, or similar system / application languages.
* Experience building and shipping production‑quality applications or services.
* Solid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and object‑oriented design.
* Experience working collaboratively in a team environment with clear communication and ownership.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience building Windows applications using WinUI, WPF, UWP, or Windows SDKs.
* Familiarity with performance optimization, memory management, and responsiveness in desktop applications.
* Experience with telemetry, experimentation, and feature flighting in consumer products.
* Exposure to accessibility (a11y) standards and inclusive design principles.
* Experience with CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, and live‑site monitoring.


**What Success Looks Like**
* Delivers polished, reliable features that improve customer satisfaction and engagement.
* Writes clean, testable code and proactively raises quality and maintainability bar.
* Demonstrates solid ownership—from design through deployment and support.
* Uses data and customer feedback to guide decisions and continuous improvement.
* Grows technical depth while positively influencing team engineering practices.


 \\#MicrosoftAI
   

  

 This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.
   

  

 Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances.  **requesting accommodations.**

title: Systems Development Engineer, Silicon
description:
**Minimum qualifications:**
* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or IT\\-related field, or equivalent practical experience.
* 3 years of experience with Linux operating systems internals and administration, technical infrastructure (e.g., deployment, maintenance, troubleshooting), and with reliability of technical infrastructure.
* 3 years of experience with one or more programming, or scripting languages (Go, Python).
* 3 years of experience with technical infrastructure (e.g., deployment, maintenance, troubleshooting).
* Experience in managing infrastructure and servers through virtualization, automation and deployments, configuration management, networking and security.


**Preferred qualifications:**
* 3 years of experience in cloud systems design.
* Understanding of technology, with the ability to deliver exceptional user experiences.
* Excellent communication, problem\\-solving, and presentation skills.


**About The Job**
 Systems Development Engineering (SDE) at Google is a role where you manage services and systems at scale. SDEs creatively put their engineering discipline to use automating the mundane and reducing toil. We don’t just write code to fix bugs, but emphasize the development of tools and solutions that fix classes of problems. We know it’s hard to control what you can’t measure – so we focus on observability: instrumenting first, then turning data into knowledge, and finally knowledge into action. We know that the operational efficiency of Google systems, services, virtual compute environments and the operating systems that power them impact the environment, not just the bottom line. We know that working together we can do more, and that community matters.
   

  

 Google brings together people with a wide variety of backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We encourage them to collaborate, think big and take risks in a blame\\-free environment. We promote self\\-direction to work on meaningful projects, while we also strive to create an environment that provides the support and mentorship needed to learn and grow.
   

  

 Together we engineer and build the infrastructure, tools, access and telemetry for systems that enable orchestration of Google\\-scale services. Come build things that matter.
   

  

 Behind everything our users see online is the architecture built by the Technical Infrastructure team to keep it running. From developing and maintaining our data centers to building the next generation of Google platforms, we make Google's product portfolio possible. We're proud to be our engineers' engineers and love voiding warranties by taking things apart so we can rebuild them. We keep our networks up and running, ensuring our users have the best and fastest experience possible.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Troubleshoot technical issues, evaluate data, and develop recommendations for systems and services within domain, respond to tickets within team\\-defined SLOs, and contribute to systems and services in related domains through bug reports and consultation.
* Participate in limited on\\-call rotation and monitoring of production distributed computing infrastructure.
* Manage the GCP project environment and VM provisioning service, ensure workstation health through Terraform debugging and adherence to established turn\\-up playbooks, work with customers on defining distributed systems requirements, testing procedures, proposing solutions.
* Implement proactive monitoring, canary update processes, and fleet\\-wide safeguards to prevent systemic failures and accidental destructive actions across the infrastructure. Provide technical troubleshooting for cloud workstations, resolve issues with access, CLI, Puppet configurations, mounts (NFS/Filestore), and disk limitations.
* Scale systems sustainably through mechanisms like automation. Evolve systems by pushing for changes that improve reliability/ velocity.


 Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

****Overview****
 Azure Storage team is chartered with building, managing, and running the persistent cloud storage for Microsoft Azure cloud. We are one of the foundational services in the Azure Cloud and host data from some of the largest companies in the world plus all of Microsoft’s largest online businesses including Xbox, Teams, and Bing.
   

  

 Azure Cloud has one of the highest number of data centers amongst all the cloud providers. Azure Storage, which is a massively scalable, highly distributed, ubiquitously accessible storage system, is designed to scale out and serve the entire world with hundreds of thousands of devices in our data centers across the world. This role provides a unique opportunity to work on projects that will help support the scale of Azure Storage to the next level. If you like the challenges of large\\-scale distributed systems, love to work on new projects where you can define the work, scope, direction, and architect new solutions to make an impact on a massive product like Azure storage, this could be a position for you! You will have an opportunity to make high impact changes on a daily basis as you build a hyperscale storage system that may indirectly or directly be used daily by your friends and family.
   

  

 We are looking for a Software Engineer II who is passionate about distributed storage, more specifically in the area of Resource Management. We are a fast\\-paced team with talented engineers. title: Software Engineer
description:
**Company Overview**
 KLA is a global leader in diversified electronics for the semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem. Virtually every electronic device in the world is produced using our technologies. No laptop, smartphone, wearable device, voice\\-controlled gadget, flexible screen, VR device or smart car would have made it into your hands without us. KLA invents systems and solutions for the manufacturing of wafers and reticles, integrated circuits, packaging, printed circuit boards and flat panel displays. The innovative ideas and devices that are advancing humanity all begin with inspiration, research and development. KLA focuses more than average on innovation and we invest 15% of sales back into R\\&D. Our expert teams of physicists, engineers, data scientists and problem\\-solvers work together with the world’s leading technology providers to accelerate the delivery of tomorrow’s electronic devices. Life here is exciting and our teams thrive on tackling really hard problems. There is never a dull moment with us.
   

  

**Group/Division**
 With over 40 years of semiconductor process control experience, chipmakers around the globe rely on KLA to ensure that their fabs ramp next\\-generation devices to volume production quickly and cost\\-effectively. Enabling the movement towards advanced chip design, KLA's Global Products Group (GPG), which is responsible for creating all of KLA’s metrology and inspection products, is looking for the best and the brightest research scientist, software engineers, application development engineers, and senior product technology process engineers. The Surfscan group includes a team of engineers, technology development, apps engineers and product marketing focused on technology that enables wafer, IC and equipment manufacturers to develop, qualify and monitor their process tools. Defects and process non\\-uniformities detected on Surfscan equipment allow for early identification of yield excursions. The flagship Surfscan products include the SPx platforms for wafer surface quality and wafer defect inspection tools and systems for inspection of polished wafers, epi wafers and engineered substrates during the wafer fabrication process.
   

  

**Job Description/Preferred Qualifications**
 We are seeking a motivated engineer to join our team to help with the design, development, release, and support our metrology products. The successful candidate will:
   

  

* Understand our ADE metrology products functionalities and the underlying DGA architecture insights to visualize the requirements from the other stake holders.
* Enhance and write software in C\\+\\+ \\& Java on Windows \\& Linux to add new and enhance the existing functionalities for the WS/PWG products.
* Design and implement features to generate different metrics using the data collected by the instrument.
* Support the customer issues and work with the stakeholders to bring to closure.
* Adhere to the development process and BKMs already in place.


**Qualifications**
* Bachelor's degree in Computer science / Electronics / Electrical / Mechanical or equivalent field (Master's preferred)
* 2 \\- 4 years of Software Engineering Experience preferrable in a product company
* Good Communication, Interpersonal and Team co\\-ordination skills required
* Self\\-learner, able to work with minimum supervision
* Creative thinker with good problem analysis and solving abilities


**Experience**
* Excellent software design/implementation capabilities
* Networking and Hardware knowledge is essential
* Technical competency in using C\\+\\+, Java, in Windows 32/64 bit programming environment
* Proven experience in using UML, OOP and OOD methodologies
* Good Debugging skills
* Proven experience in User interface design and implementation
* Semi\\-conductor domain experience is a plus


**Preferred Experience:**
* Knowledge in working with distributed, cross platform (Windows\\-Linux) application
* Knowledge of KT specific or general semiconductor domain
* Minimum Qualifications: Master's Level Degree or work experience of 0\\-1 year, Bachelor's Level Degree or work experience of 2\\-4 years


**Minimum Qualifications**
 Master's Level Degree or work experience of 0\\-1 year, Bachelor's Level Degree or work experience of 2\\-4 years
   

  

*We offer a competitive, family friendly total rewards package. We design our programs to reflect our commitment to an inclusive environment, while ensuring we provide benefits that meet the diverse needs of our employees.*
*KLA is proud to be an equal opportunity employer*
 Be aware of potentially fraudulent job postings or suspicious recruiting activity by persons that are currently posing as KLA employees. KLA never asks for any financial compensation to be considered for an interview, to become an employee, or for equipment. Further, KLA does not work with any recruiters or third parties who charge such fees either directly or on behalf of KLA. Please ensure that you have searched KLA’s Careers website for legitimate job postings. KLA follows a recruiting process that involves multiple interviews in person or on video conferencing with our hiring managers. If you are concerned that a communication, an interview, an offer of employment, or that an employee is not legitimate, please send an email to talent.acquisition@kla.com to confirm the person you are communicating with is an employee. We take your privacy very seriously and confidentially handle your information.

title: Software Engineer
description:
**About Us**
 Automation Anywhere is the leader in Agentic Process Automation (APA), transforming how work gets done with AI\\-powered automation. Its APA system, built on the industry’s first Process Reasoning Engine (PRE) and specialized AI agents, combines process discovery, RPA, end\\-to\\-end orchestration, document processing, and analytics—all delivered with enterprise\\-grade security and governance. **Overview**

 **Junior AI/ML Engineer** 
 to join our
 **Sustaining Engineering** 
 team. This role is ideal for early‑career engineers who enjoy solving real\\-world technical challenges, working directly on customer issues, and building intelligent tools that improve developer productivity and operational efficiency. The engineer should thrive on solving complex customer issues and ensuring production systems run smoothly. In this role, you'll be the bridge between our customers and internal teams, combining technical expertise with strong communication skills to maintain the trust our customers place in us. The engineer will contribute not only by analyzing, diagnosing, and resolving customer‑reported issues, but also by creating
 **AI\\-driven and agentic solutions** 
 that streamline engineering workflows, automate troubleshooting, and enhance log/code analysis capabilities across the organization.
   

  

 Location \\- Bangalore
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Customer Issue Analysis \\& Troubleshooting

* AI/ML Tooling \\& Agentic Solution Development
* Design, build \\& maintain AI\\-powered tools, including:
+ Automated log analysis assistants
+ Code‑understanding agents
+ Troubleshooting copilots
+ Root\\-cause analysis (RCA) recommender systems

* Develop agentic workflows to improve engineering productivity, such as:
+ Intelligent search over logs and codebases
+ Automated test generation
+ Impact analysis of code changes
+ Support bots to assist sustaining engineers

* Continuously explore new models, frameworks, and techniques to enhance the team’s capabilities.
* Technical Troubleshooting \\& Resolution
* Investigate and diagnose complex issues across the full stack using AI\\-powered observability tools and traditional debugging techniques
* Dive deep into codebases to identify root causes of production incidents

* Support release processes by validating fixes, contributing to hotfixes or service packs as needed.
* Proactively look for opportunities to reduce manual effort through intelligent automation.
* Quality \\& Continuous Improvement
* Analyze patterns in customer issues to identify opportunities for product improvements
* Contribute to building stronger observability and monitoring capabilities
* Document troubleshooting processes and solutions to build a knowledge base for the team
* Participate in post\\-incident reviews with a learning mindset, focusing on prevention and process improvements
* Cross\\-Functional Collaboration
* Work closely with Support, CloudOps, Product, and Engineering teams to deliver fast, reliable resolutions
* Advocate for customer needs within internal teams while balancing technical constraints
* Share insights from customer interactions to inform product roadmap and quality initiatives


**Skills \\& Qualifications**
**Technical Skills**
* 2\\-3 years of professional experience in product development
* Solid foundation in Python, with experience in ML/AI libraries such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit‑learn, or Transformers\\-based frameworks.
* Good understanding of software engineering concepts: debugging, logging, REST APIs, distributed systems basics.
* Understanding of web application architecture, APIs, and databases
* Familiarity with LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG systems, or agentic workflows is a strong plus.
* Basic knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure or GCP).
* Experience with Git, CI/CD, and modern engineering practices.
* Strong programming skills with experience debugging and fixing production code
* Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot complex technical issues systematically
* Experience working with version control systems (Git) and creating pull requests
* Genuine passion for customer success and problem\\-solving
* Familiarity with observability and monitoring tools
* Experience with cloud platforms and distributed systems is a plus


**Soft Skills \\& Attributes**
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to various audiences
* Strong sense of ownership and accountability for customer outcomes
* Empathy and patience when working with frustrated customers in high\\-pressure situations
* Collaborative mindset with the ability to work effectively across teams
* Growth mindset with eagerness to learn from every challenge
* Self\\-motivated with strong organizational and follow\\-up skills
* Strong aptitude for diagnosing technical issues through log analysis, code review, and experimentation.
* Curiosity\\-driven mindset with the ability to think systematically through complex problems.
* Ability to learn fast and adapt to rapidly evolving technologies.
* High ownership mindset and inclination for continuous improvement.


**Our Culture**
 We operate with one guiding principle:
 **Customer and partner trust is our \\#1 priority** 
 . Every decision we make, every issue we resolve, and every interaction we have is guided by this commitment.
   

  

 We foster a culture of ownership and learning where challenges become opportunities to strengthen customer relationships and improve our products. We measure what matters, leverage AI\\-powered tools to work smarter, and continuously raise the bar for quality and reliability.
   

  

**Why Join Us**

description:

 The massive data proliferation over the last decades has revealed the limitations of many legal technology products, impacting the efficiency of legal practices. title: System Engineer
description:
**Job Description Summary**
 Systems engineering is a cross\\-functional engineering discipline centered on an approach, mindset, and process. All activities that consider both the business and the technical needs of the customers with the goal of providing a quality product that meets the users needs. The discipline that executes a robust process of design, creation, and optimization of systems, consisting of identification and quantification of system requirements, creation of alternate system design concepts, performance of design trade studies, selection and implementation of the best designs and verification that the design is properly integrated and executed. Process that proceeds from concept to production to operation.
   

  

 Impacts quality of own work and the work of others on the team. Executes standard operational/technical tasks typically subject to instructions and work routines. There is latitude to rearrange the sequence to complete task/duties based on changing work situations.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Essential Responsibilities:
   

  

* Collaborate with system and subsystem leads to define and develop top level control system \\& software requirements, design and integrate secure network architectures for O\\&G/ Industry/ Marine projects.
* Ensure all projects are compliant to Cyber\\-security requirements.
* Be able to scope and participate in hardware and software vulnerability identification and vulnerability risk assessment
* Engage in application and domain\\-specific threat modeling and attack surface analysis/reduction
* Lead or assist in the investigation of security alerts and coordinate the response to contain and mitigate threats.
* Ensure documentation of technical data generated for the assigned product or project and/or tasks are in line with policies and procedures.
* Engage in Network Device Hardening/ OS Hardening/ Protocol Hardening and verifying hardening against IEC 62443\\-4\\-2
* Ensure all projects comply with industry standards and regulations, such as IEC 62443, NIST SP 800\\-82, ISO 27001, and relevant regional mandates (e.g., TSA Security Directives or NERC CIP where applicable).
* Perform Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT) and Site Acceptance Testing (SAT) from a cybersecurity perspective.
* O\\&G/ Industry/ Marine projects involve many vendors (EPCs, OEMs, service providers). You will audit and manage the secure "onboarding" of vendor equipment and remote support tools.
* Advise tendering and project team on cybersecurity budget requirements and technical road maps.

* Drive lean mind set to provide optimum technical solutions for the projects.

**Qualifications / Requirements**
* Bachelor /master’s degree in computer science/ electrical/ electronic or relevant engineering from reputed College/University.

description:

**About The Team**

We build, expand and extend outstanding database, key\\-value and object storage applications to store and orchestrate documents in a SaaS cloud environment. Our team's ownership is focused primarily on private data centers services but with an eye on growing the public cloud ownership also.
   

  

**About The Role**
* As a Senior Software Engineer on the Document Persistent Storage team, you will own the design, implementation, and scaling of our Erlang/Riak\\-based storage service, as well as the next generation of persistence\\-layer applications that will underpin our platform.
* You have a strong knowledge of distributed systems including Service Oriented Architecture, multi\\-system application deployments, distributed data processing/management, clustering, networking, and load balancing. You are able to design services and solutions that process requests/data on many servers, are loosely coupled, scale horizontally, and are deployed globally.
* We are looking for detailed understanding and application of low level operating systems concepts including multi\\-threading, concurrency, memory management, networking and storage, performance and scale. Proficient in identifying bottlenecks and optimizing performance.
* You perform well with minimum guidance and demonstrate proficiency in identifying and making improvements to scalability, reliability, service health, supportability, observability and manageability of the product and you implement resilience techniques like timeouts, circuit breaking, failover, retries, etc
* You focus on defining clear, robust abstractions and modular architectures, while also designing well\\-structured programmatic interfaces that enable customers and internal services to integrate reliably at scale.
* You can harness Erlang, Elixir, OTP, and modern distributed database computing technologies to deliver fault\\-tolerant, low\\-latency, and highly scalable databases storage solutions.


**About You**
 Basic Qualifications
   

  

* 8\\+ years of proven experience designing and building large scale distributed database systems using open source technologies.
* 3\\+ years of demonstrated experience coding in Erlang, Elixir or other similar languages.
* Proficiency in debugging skills.


**Other Qualifications**
* Deep understanding of Linux networking and storage layers.
* Experience and understanding of the internals of at least one distributed Database system such as Riak, Hadoop, Spark, HBase, Cassandra, Kafka, etc.
* Master's or PhD degree in computer Science or equivalent experience.


**Our Approach to Flexible Work**
 With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in\\-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in\\-office each week, we simply
 **spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field** 
 with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.
   

  

 At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email accommodations@workday.com.

title: Applied Scientist II
description:
**Overview**
 Follow\\-on Suggestions are prompts that appear after an AI assistant or search engine, such as Copilot or Bing, responds to a user query. These suggestions, including features like "People Also Ask" and "Related Searches" in Bing, help users continue their search, complete tasks, and explore related topics efficiently. The Follow\\-on Suggestions team within STCI, part of the Microsoft AI (MAI) organization, is responsible for developing these features for Copilot and Bing.
   

  

 The team is seeking a motivated and self\\-driven Applied Scientist II with a passion for working on machine learning and deep learning models at internet scale. The ideal candidate will possess strong ML/DL skills, practical experience with real\\-world problems, and expertise in enhancing products through advanced machine learning techniques. Familiarity with one or more ML/DL frameworks, a deep understanding of algorithms, and hands\\-on solution implementation are crucial. The position requires excellence in scientific thinking and execution, efficient experiment definition, development of state\\-of\\-the\\-art algorithms, and broad NLP engineering problem\\-solving abilities. We value individuals motivated to tackle challenging issues, eager to work within a large team of data scientists and engineers, and ready to make an impact on how the web operates.
   

  

 Our team comprises engineers, scientists, and program managers from diverse backgrounds and experiences, united by a shared vision to make the world smarter and more productive. We believe that inclusive teams, focused on customer needs and mutual trust, are essential for creating outstanding products. Collaboration across the company enables us to leverage technological breakthroughs and integrate AI into various Microsoft products. For a glimpse into our team culture, visit our Instagram page (@lifeatstci). We encourage you to learn more about this role and apply!

As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.
   

  

 Starting January 26, 2026, Microsoft AI (MAI) employees who live within a 50\\-mile commute of a designated Microsoft office in the U.S. or 25\\-mile commute of a non\\-U.S., country\\-specific location are expected to work from the office at least four days per week. This expectation is subject to local law and may vary by jurisdiction.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Develop novel machine learning and data mining algorithms, and fine\\-tune LLM/SLMs to support follow\\-on suggestion experiences in Copilot and Bing.
* Create reusable modules with data quality checks for model training and evaluation.
* Host ML models and manage model implementation using production\\-ready, optimized code, evaluating trade\\-offs between cost, performance, latency, and throughput to determine optimal operating points.

**Qualifications**

* Strong research and/or development background in architecting, developing, and delivering advanced machine learning, deep learning NLP projects is preferred.
* Minimum 2 years of hands\\-on experience with ML frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, HuggingFace, and ONNX.
* Robust data analysis and data mining skills using Big Data frameworks like Spark and Hadoop/MapReduce.
* Excellent problem\\-solving, communication, and collaboration skills.
* Customer\\-focused, strategic, results\\-driven, self\\-motivated, and enthusiastic about working in a diverse team and collaborating across geographies.
* Proven track record of successful delivery of large\\-scale applied ML projects in an industry setting.

description:

title: Technical Manager
description:
Job Title: Technical Manager – Automation
   

  

 Location: Chennai (Mode: Hybrid)
   

  

 Company: Epiroc Mining India Pvt. Ltd
   

  

**About Epiroc**
 Epiroc is a global leader in mining and infrastructure equipment, dedicated to driving productivity and sustainability across the industry. We are committed to building a better, more sustainable world through cutting\\-edge technologies and innovative solutions in automation, digitalization, and electrification.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are seeking a Technical Manager – Automation, to provide strong technical leadership and system\\-level direction for developing advanced automation solutions in Epiroc’s Underground portfolio.
   

  

 This role is focused on heavy machinery automation \\- encompassing embedded control systems, real\\-time communication, and offboard supervisory platforms for mining and construction equipment.
   

  

 You will act as a technical steward, driving design integrity, software\\-hardware integration, and cross\\-functional alignment across Underground Material Handling, Drilling, and brand\\-agnostic automation platforms.
   

  

 You will play a pivotal role in shaping next\\-generation automation and control architectures \\- including Automotive CAN\\-based systems, C/C\\+\\+ development, and Qt\\-based visualization \\- ensuring scalable, reliable, and safe operation of Epiroc’s autonomous and semi\\-autonomous equipment.
   

  

 This position requires deep hands\\-on technical expertise, system\\-level thinking, and collaboration across global R\\&D teams.
   

  

 Mission of the Role
   

  

 To define and drive the technical direction, architecture, and integration of automation and control systems that enable Epiroc’s next generation of intelligent and sustainable mining \\& construction equipment.
   

  

 This role focuses on developing robust, scalable, and interoperable system architectures across onboard and offboard automation domains \\- ensuring seamless interaction between embedded controllers, sensors, and operator platforms.
   

  

 By combining deep system expertise, architectural stewardship, and cross\\-domain collaboration, the Technical Manager ensures that every automation solution is technically robust and future\\-ready while supporting new technology initiatives, prototypes, and proof\\-of\\-concepts to align with global divisional R\\&D teams, accelerate time\\-to\\-market, and drive business growth in line with Epiroc’s sustainability and innovation goals.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
**Technical Leadership:**
 Define and guide the system, hardware, and software architecture for onboard (embedded) and offboard automation solutions.
   

  

 Ensure scalability, interoperability, and reusability across control systems, platforms, and global projects.
   

  

 System Integration:
   

  

 Lead the integration of embedded controllers, sensors, HMIs, and supervisory systems, ensuring seamless system behavior and communication.
   

  

 Oversee implementation using C/C\\+\\+, Qt/QML, and Automotive CAN/J1939 frameworks, maintaining high standards of performance and safety.
   

  

 Drive hardware–software co\\-design efforts, collaborating with electrical and mechanical engineers to ensure cohesive system performance and optimized interfaces.
   

  

 Conduct design and code reviews to ensure compliance with architectural principles, functional safety standards (ISO 13849 / IEC 61508\\), and software best practices.
   

  

 Innovation:
   

  

 Support new technology initiatives, prototypes, and proof\\-of\\-concepts that strengthen future product capabilities.
   

  

 Drive continuous improvement initiatives that enhance system reliability, maintainability, and efficiency.
   

  

 Project Execution:
   

  

 Oversee validation and testing of automation features through simulation, lab setups, and field trials, ensuring hardware–software reliability and performance.
   

  

 Collaborate with global test and verification teams to ensure system readiness and compliance with safety and quality standards.
   

  

 Technical Governance and Knowledge Sharing:
   

  

 Establish and uphold design standards, coding guidelines, and version\\-control practices across projects.
   

  

 Promote DevOps and CI/CD methodologies for efficient software delivery and integration.
   

  

 Mentor engineers by sharing architectural and hardware integration insights, fostering a culture of learning, collaboration, and technical excellence.
   

  

 Collaboration:
   

  

 Work closely with global R\\&D centers to align architecture, feature roadmaps, and software / hardware standards.
   

  

 Collaborate with mechanical, electrical, and testing teams to ensure system coherence and end\\-to\\-end performance validation.
   

  

 Serve as a technical bridge between product management, controls, and software teams, ensuring consistent design intent.
   

  

 Required Skills and Qualifications:
   

  

**Technical Expertise:**
 Proficiency in C and C\\+\\+ (11/14/17\\) for embedded or real\\-time systems.
   

  

 Strong experience with Automotive CAN / J1939 communication and diagnostics.
   

  

 Hands\\-on experience in Qt/QML for HMI or visualization development.
   

  

 System\\-level understanding of distributed architectures, controllers, sensors, and HMIs.
   

  

 Familiarity with build systems (CMake), version control (Git), and CI/CD environments.
   

  

 Architectural and System Integration Skills:
   

  

 Demonstrated experience in defining and evolving systems or software architectures for complex embedded systems.
   

  

 Proven ability to bridge hardware–software integration, ensuring interoperability across platforms and components.
   

  

 Deep understanding of functional decomposition, interface design, and system behavior validation.
   

  

 Collaboration and Technical Influence:
   

  

 Strong ability to influence technical decisions and align cross\\-functional teams toward a common architecture or system goal.
   

  

 Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills across multicultural, global teams.
   

  

 Capable of mentoring engineers and guiding design discussions to maintain architectural consistency and technical excellence.
   

  

**Domain Experience:**
 Prior experience in Mining, Construction, Off\\-highway, or Automotive industries preferred.
   

  

 Exposure to Functional Safety standards (ISO 13849 / IEC 61508\\) is an advantage.
   

  

 Education:
   

  

 Bachelor’s or master’s degree in Mechatronics, Robotics, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science Engineering or a related field.
   

  

**Nice to Have:**
 Experience with SCADA, remote control, tele\\-operation, or autonomous machinery used in heavy equipment or off\\-highway vehicles.
   

  

 Knowledge of functional safety (ISO 13849 / IEC 61508\\) and cybersecurity concepts for embedded control systems.
   

  

 Familiarity with simulation and testing tools (e.g., Gazebo, Unity, or MATLAB/Simulink\\-based environments) for validation of automation features.
   

  

 Experience with hardware\\-in\\-the\\-loop (HIL) or software\\-in\\-the\\-loop (SIL) testing setups.
   

  

 Exposure to system engineering processes (requirements management, system verification, and validation).’
   

  

 Prior collaboration with global cross\\-functional R\\&D teams or international assignments.
   

  

 Certification such as Certified Functional Safety Engineer (TÜV SÜD) is an advantage
   

  

**What We Offer:**
* Competitive Salary and Benefits: Enjoy a rewarding compensation package tailored to your expertise.
* Global career opportunities across Epiroc’s worldwide network.
* Epiroc University for continuous learning and competence development.
* Competitive salary and benefits, rewarding your expertise and contribution.
* Innovative projects that shape the future of automation and sustainability.
* Hybrid work culture supporting flexibility, collaboration, and well\\-being.
* Inclusive environment built on trust, innovation, and teamwork.


 Why you should apply: This role offers high technical visibility, cross\\-functional collaboration, and the opportunity to shape the architecture and integration of next\\-generation automation and control systems across onboard and offboard domains. You’ll collaborate with global R\\&D teams to define scalable, interoperable, and brand\\-agnostic automation platforms that enable seamless operation across mixed fleets in mining and construction \\- strengthening Epiroc’s global leadership in automation and autonomy. If you’re passionate about driving system\\-level innovation, ensuring design integrity, and influencing how intelligent and sustainable machines are built, this position provides the perfect platform to make a real\\-world impact.
   

  

**It all starts with people.** 
 The world needs metals and minerals for the energy transition and our cities and infrastructure must be developed to serve a growing population. To succeed, we need to speed up the shift towards more sustainable mining and construction industries. We at Epiroc accelerate this transformation, together with customers and business partners in more than 150 countries, by developing and providing innovative and safe equipment, digital solutions, and aftermarket support.
   

  

 All new thinkers are welcome. We are looking for those who want to develop, grow, and dare to think new. In Epiroc we attract, develop, and retain diverse talent valuing authenticity and unique perspectives, driving our spirit of innovation. We foster an inclusive culture where diversity isn't just a goal but a part of our values and way of working. This is how we do business for a sustainable future.  Learn more at www.epiroc.com

title: Senior Cyber Security Engineer
description:
**As a Sr Cybersecurity Engineer at Cloud Software Group, you will design and implement the technical safeguards that protect our people and platforms. You will partner with Engineering, IT, and diverse Business Units to build scalable security workflows that align with business needs.**
**You will drive the full lifecycle of our security tooling—from initial assessment and implementation to continuous tuning and optimization. Join us to move beyond reactive security and help engineer the future of our proactive cyber defense program.**

* Create and maintain clear technical documentation for security configurations, processes, and improvements.
* Actively monitor global threat intelligence feeds and vulnerability disclosures to identify emerging risks and integrate proactive countermeasures into our security architecture.

**Required Experience/Skills**

* Deep technical understanding of security controls across multiple domains, including Vulnerability Lifecycle Management, Privileged Access Management (PAM), Identity \\& Access Management (IAM), and Network Defense.
* Knowledge of one or more Cloud platforms (AWS, Azure and GCP) and best practices for architecting and securing.
* Familiarity with security telemetry concepts, including logging, monitoring, and alerting.

****Nice to Have / Growth Areas****

title: Platform Engineering Associate – D&A Product Reliability & Operations (GCP / DevOps/ SRE)
description:
**Job Description**
**Are You Ready to Make It Happen at Mondelēz International?**
**Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It Uniquely Yours.**
 Platform Engineering Associate – D\\&A Product Reliability \\& Operations (GCP / DevOps / SRE)
   

  

 Experience: 4\\+ years
   

  

 Purpose: Support, operate, and continuously improve Data \\& Analytics (D\\&A) products running on Google Cloud (GCP) by applying a platform\\-as\\-a\\-product mindset to run\\-state ownership. Drive reliability, security, observability, and cost\\-aware operations through hands\\-on engineering (Terraform, CI/CD, monitoring, automation) and disciplined incident management.
   

  

 Core Responsibilities (Product Ownership \\+ Run\\-State):
   

  

 Own day\\-to\\-day platform operational health for a defined portfolio of D\\&A products (pipelines, data products, analytics apps/dashboards, ML workloads). Manage the run backlog: intake, triage, prioritization, resolution, and prevention.
   

  

 Establish and maintain runbooks, operational readiness checklists, SLAs/SLOs where applicable, and clear support documentation to enable self\\-service and reduce tickets.
   

  

 Apply SRE practices: define/track SLIs/SLOs (availability, data freshness/latency, job success rate, quality signals), participate in on\\-call/incident response, lead structured triage, and drive post\\-incident corrective actions to closure.
   

  

 Build and maintain observability: dashboards and actionable alerts across logs/metrics/traces and D\\&A signals (pipeline failures, SLA misses, anomalies). Reduce alert noise and improve MTTR through better instrumentation and automation.
   

  

 Triage and remediate security vulnerabilities across product runtimes (images, libraries, pipelines, IaC). Embed security checks and compliance controls into CI/CD and support audit evidence needs.
   

  

 Support infrastructure and environment consistency via Terraform and GCP services (IAM, networking, Compute/GKE, Storage, Monitoring/Logging).
   

  

 Integrate FinOps fundamentals into operations: enforce tagging/labeling, identify waste (idle/oversized resources, runaway jobs), and partner with FinOps/product owners to implement optimizations.
   

  

 Required Skills: Terraform (PR\\-based governance), strong GCP fundamentals, Kubernetes/GKE familiarity preferred, CI/CD (GitHub Actions/Jenkins), observability (Cloud Monitoring/Logging and/or Prometheus/Grafana/Datadog), security tooling exposure (Dependabot/GitHub Advanced Security, SonarQube, Wiz/Tenable), Python/Bash automation, strong troubleshooting and stakeholder communication.
